entries.content:
- React Strict Mode double-mount in dev causes `availableDatesFetchedRef` to block the dates fetch — fixed with `availableDates.length > 0` guard
- Response Map needs base map + dates API to return data before it renders — if either fails silently, map shows infinite spinner
- Labeling requires BOTH Anthropic (brands) AND OpenAI (sentiment) keys — if either is missing/expired, responses stay unlabeled
- BrightData scraping creates responses but labeling is a separate Inngest job — responses can

1. Manual prompt run not implemented (needs /api/scrape/trigger endpoint)
2. No error tracking SDK (Sentry) — operational concern
3. In-memory rate limiting won't persist across serverless instances
4. Position tracking fields zeroed (schema doesn't have position data)
5. Dashboard home page is a placeholder (static cards, no metrics)
6. Model filter on Responses page tracks state but doesn't filter data
7. Dead duplicate processStripeWebhook in lib/inngest/functions.ts
8. Some email domains sti
Push to `main` on `nu-agent-uppababy` triggers immediate Cloud Run (backend) + Vercel (frontend) deploy. **No staging gate.** Test locally before pushing.

**Why:** When campaigns fail (ET5 batch errors or ET14 timeout), reserved external coupons were stranded in `orch_external_coupon_reservation` and never released back to the pool.

**How to apply:** These changes are on branch `PME-1460`. Reference when working on any coupon reservation/unreservation logic.

### Productization (Round 1 — 5 agents)
- Settings queries migrated from legacy tables (org_members → memberships, org_invites → invites)
- Prompt CRUD API (POST/PATCH/DELETE/bulk) implemented
- Response analytics grounded (removed Math.random, wired real data)
- Billing hardened (removed placeholder email, price ID validation, subscription status)
- 4 CRITICAL security fixes (org isolation in /api/improve/* routes)

### Quality Audit (Round 2 — 5 agents)
- 7 bugs fixed (empty .in() data leaks,

The agent backend session is NOT linked to Shopware in cross-origin local dev. The `SessionInjectorMiddleware` requires `x-nulogic-sid` header (only set by Nuxt server middleware in same-origin mode). The `session/init` endpoint injects the token but the A2A handler uses a different session context. This is why the synthetic checkout fallback exists — it bypasses the backend entirely for storefront-synced carts.

- `/api/references` — Citation analytics (drives Overview tab)
- `/api/references/map` — Response Map graph data
- `/api/references/dates` — Available dates for date picker
- `/api/references/brand-report` — Brand Report + AI Insights data
- `/api/references/heatmap` — Heatmap + Scorecard data
- `/api/references/improvement-plan` — Improvement Plan recommendations

**Why:** Understanding the architecture prevents re-discovering file locations and avoids legacy table name mistakes.
**How to apply

- **`storefront-sync` sentinel ID**: synthetic checkout uses `id: 'storefront-sync'` to distinguish from backend-provided checkouts. All CRUD and checkout button handlers check this to route correctly.
- **Dual channel**: every message sent via BroadcastChannel AND postMessage to cover both same-origin (production) and cross-origin (local dev).
- **Price convention**: storefront sends decimal line totals (e.g., 5399.94 for 6×899.99). Agent `Checkout` type uses cents unit prices (89999). Syntheti

NU-AURA is a **bundle app platform** with 4 sub-applications:
- **NU-HRMS** (~98%) — 261 pages, 170+ controllers, 360 entities — production-ready
- **NU-Hire** (~97%) — Recruitment, agencies, scorecards (new API), onboarding, career page, e-sign
- **NU-Grow** (~92%) — Reviews, OKRs, 360 feedback, LMS, training, surveys, wellness
- **NU-Fluence** (~90%) — Wiki (18 routes, 30+ components), blogs, templates, search, AI chat, wall

**Codebase:** 261 routes | 170+ controllers | 206+ services | 360+ e
8 tabs: Response Map, Overview, Response List, Brand Report, AI Insights, Heatmap, Scorecard, Improvement Plan

The agent chat widget is embedded as an **iframe** inside the Nuxt storefront.

**Production (same-origin):** Agent served at `uppababy.com/chat/` via Nuxt `routeRules` proxy. BroadcastChannel works.

**Local dev (cross-origin):** Agent at `localhost:3009`, storefront at `localhost:3000`. Uses postMessage fallback.

### Key bridge files:
- `uppababy-migration-fe/plugins/agent-bridge.client.ts` — BroadcastChannel bridge (storefront side)
- `uppababy-migration-fe/composables/useAgentBridgeSync.ts`

- [NU-Hire + NU-Fluence Gap Closure](project_hire_fluence_gaps_2026_04_05.md) — Agencies, inline comments, wall trending, all gaps closed
- [Design System Overhaul](project_design_system_overhaul.md) — Blue monochrome + skeuomorphism + full consistency sweep
- [Sonar Issue Fixes](project_sonar_fixes_2026_03_30.md) — Lint cleanup + Sonar remediation across frontend & backend

- `organizations` — billing_tier, stripe_customer_id, subscription_status
- `memberships` — user_id, org_id, role (NOT "org_members" — that's legacy)
- `invites` — email, token, accepted_at (NOT "org_invites" — that's legacy)
- `volumes` — monitoring units (one brand per volume)
- `prompts` — questions sent to AI models
- `responses` — AI answers with brands[], sentiment, cited_domains[], response_references JSONB
- `scrape_runs` — execution tracking
- `tracked_brands` — primary + competitor bra

In `uppababy-migration-fe/.env`:
- `NUXT_PUBLIC_AGENT_URL="http://localhost:3009"` — forces cross-origin iframe (set to `""` for same-origin via `/chat/` proxy)
- `AGENT_BACKEND_URL` — Python backend URL (default: `http://localhost:11003`)

In `nu-agent-uppababy/frontend`:
- `VITE_BACKEND_PORT` — Python backend port (default: 11003)
- `VITE_BASE_URL` — Set to `/chat/` when running behind Nuxt proxy

entries.content:
1. **Nested Page Hierarchy** — DONE. Tree queries, breadcrumbs, movePage, WikiPageTree component.
2. **Per-Space Permissions** — DONE. V117 migration, SpaceMember entity, SpacePermissionsDrawer.
3. **PDF/Word Export** — DONE. WikiExportService, export button in wiki view.
4. **Inline Comments** — DONE. V119 migration, WikiInlineComment entity, REST API, InlineCommentsPanel component (text selection, threaded replies, resolve/delete), integrated into wiki page view.
5. **Macros** — DONE. MacroRen

### Backend Fixes
1. **Exception swallowing (P0)**: Added SLF4J logging to 8 silent catch blocks across 5 files (EmployeeDashboardService, AttendanceController, ContractLifecycleScheduler, KekaMigrationService, AttendanceImportService)
2. **Public fields (P2)**: PaymentGatewayAdapter inner classes — fields made private, added `@Data`, updated 33 accessor calls across 4 files
3. **Wildcard imports (P2)**: Replaced `import java.util.*` with explicit imports in 5 security/core files (JwtTokenProvid

- Don't use `org_members`, `org_invites`, `org_invite_links` — they're legacy. Use `memberships` and `invites`
- Don't add `?skip=flow` bypasses — was flagged as production security issue
- Don't query Supabase directly from client components — use API routes for org isolation
- Don't assume Next.js 16 patterns — this is Next.js 14 (no proxy.ts, no async searchParams)
- Don't use `.svg` for Perplexity logo — only `.png` exists
- Don't cache empty map results — leads to "map looks broken" during

- `app/api/` — API routes (all org-scoped via getOrgContext)
- `app/(dashboard)/dashboard/` — Dashboard pages (responses, sources, products, improve, prompts, settings)
- `components/references/map-tab.tsx` — 5000+ line Response Map component (very large, edit carefully)
- `lib/auth/org-context.ts` — Auth helper (derives org_id from memberships, never from request)
- `lib/supabase/middleware.ts` — Flow state routing (no_org → onboarding, scrape_running → progress, ready → dashboard)
- `lib/innge

- `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY` + `NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY` + `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET`

Complete migration from mixed-palette (Lapis Blue/Teal/Purple) to blue monochrome (hue ~228) with skeuomorphism.

**Why:** The original palette mixed teal, purple, and blue creating visual inconsistency. The user wanted a "simple clean elegant professional" look following NULogic's blue branding.

**How to apply:** All frontend colors, shadows, borders, backgrounds, and spacing are defined in `frontend/app/globals.css` CSS variables. Components reference these via `var(--token)` or Tailwind toke

**Goal:** NU-AURA production-ready with complete RBAC + core features working. Option B+C from grill-me session.

**Plan file:** `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-01-platform-readiness-rbac-features.md`

### Key Decisions

1. **HR_ADMIN** is a real backend role (rank 85, between TENANT_ADMIN and HR_MANAGER). Needs Flyway V94 + RoleHierarchy.java update.
2. **Permission canonical source:** `Permission.java` (backend). Frontend derives from it. DB format: `module.action`. Frontend normalizes to `MOD

### What NU-AURA Actually Is
**NU-AURA is an internal platform**, not a SaaS product for sale. It provides shared SSO, RBAC (500+ permissions), and common infrastructure for multiple internal applications at NULogic.

**Why:** Previously misunderstood as a multi-tenant SaaS product. Corrected by the user: this is built by NULogic, for NULogic.

### Why NU-HRMS Exists
NULogic currently pays for KEKA. NU-HRMS is being built as a direct replacement to eliminate that cost. This is the #1 priority —

### QA+DEV Loop Results (3 Phases)

#### Phase 1 — Page Rendering (Super Admin)
- 55 pages browser-tested + 45 API endpoints via curl
- 8 bugs found and fixed (4 frontend, 4 backend)
- 0 remaining failures

#### Phase 2 — RBAC (6 roles × 32 endpoints)
- Super Admin: 49/49 pass
- Tenant Admin: 32/32 pass
- HR Admin: 28/32 pass (4 fixed)
- HR Manager: 26/32 pass (3 fixed)
- Team Lead: 14/32 pass (permission gaps — many expected)
- Employee: 30/31 pass (contract data leak fixed)
- 5 RBAC bugs fixed

**File:** `docs/qa/NU-AURA-QA-USE-CASES.md` (6,900+ lines, 349 use cases)
**RBAC Test Plan:** `docs/TEST-PLAN-RBAC-USECASES.md` (130+ RBAC-specific checks across 6 roles)

### Execution Status (as of 2026-04-08)

| Phase | Tested | Pass | Fail | Blocked |
|-------|--------|------|------|---------|
| Phase 1 (Pages) | 55 browser + 45 curl | 53 | 2 (fixed) | 0 |
| Phase 2 (RBAC) | 207 checks across 6 roles | 179 | 20 (5 fixed) | 8 |
| Phase 3 (Use Cases) | 93/349 | 51 | 22 | 12 |

### Result Files

Fixed ~90 ESLint warnings across 44 frontend files: - **Unused imports/variables**: 32 files, ~70 items removed - **React hooks exhaustive-deps**: 6 files, wrapped logical expressions in `useMemo` - **`` → `next/image`**: 6 files, 10 replacements (with `unoptimized` for external URLs) - **Design grid violations**: 5 files, `p-3`→`p-4`, `gap-3`→`gap-4` (8px grid) **Why:** ESLint was reporting ~90 warnings. All resolved to zero.

- Use parallel agents in isolated worktrees for independent work streams
- Always run typecheck + test + build before committing
- Clear .next cache when webpack errors appear (rm -rf .next)
- Use `pnpm dev:full` not just `pnpm dev` — Inngest dev server needed for background jobs
- Check which volume/org the dashboard is showing — user has multiple
- Seed test data when API keys aren't available to unblock testing
- Make tooltips and labels business-friendly, not technical

- **Framework**: Next.js 14.2.28 (App Router) — NOT 16, don't use proxy.ts or async searchParams
- **Database**: Supabase (Postgres + Auth + RLS)
- **Payments**: Stripe (checkout, portal, webhooks, metered billing)
- **Background Jobs**: Inngest (scraping, labeling, email notifications)
- **Scraping**: BrightData bulk API (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini, Grok)
- **AI**: Anthropic Claude (brand analysis, labeling), OpenAI (sentiment via logprobs)
- **Email**: Resend
- **Charts**: Recharts
- **Icons*

Fixed full bidirectional cart sync between the UPPAbaby Shopware storefront and the NULogic AI agent chat widget, including CRUD operations and checkout flow.

**Why:** Cart updates on the storefront weren't reflecting in the agent chat, and vice versa. Checkout from the agent showed "cart is empty" on new sessions.

**How to apply:** Clean changelist is in `CART-SYNC-CHANGES.md` at the project root (`/Users/fayaz.m/IdeaProjects/nulogic/uppababy/CART-SYNC-CHANGES.md`). It lists only the cart syn

This is a multi-repo platform. The `uppababy` folder contains:

| Directory | Purpose | Tech |
|-----------|---------|------|
| `uppababy-migration-fe/` | Shopware 6 storefront (Nuxt 3) | Vue/Nuxt, Shopware Frontends |
| `nu-agent-uppababy/` | UPPAbaby-specific agent (brand config + frontend) | Python backend (FastAPI) + React frontend (Vite) |
| `nu-shopping-agent/` | Core platform engine (shared across all brands) | Python |
| `uppababy-migration-be/` | Shopware 6 backend plugins | PHP |

name: Always work on main branch
description: User prefers all commits directly on main branch — do not create feature branches
type: feedback
---

Always commit directly to `main` branch. Do not create feature branches unless explicitly asked.

**Why:** User prefers a simpler workflow without branch management overhead.

**How to apply:** Skip the branch creation step in commit workflows. Commit directly to whatever branch is current (which should be `main`).

name: Compact desktop-first UI sizing
description: NU-AURA is desktop-first — never apply mobile 44px touch targets or inflate component sizes based on mobile UX audits
type: feedback
---

Always use compact, professional sizing for NU-AURA components. This is a desktop-first internal HR tool.

**Why:** Generic UX audit tools apply mobile/consumer app standards (44px touch targets, larger text) that make NU-AURA look bloated and unprofessional. User explicitly rejected inflated sizes after an au

name: Always allow git index.lock removal
description: Always remove .git/index.lock without asking — multi-agent workflows create stale locks
type: feedback
originSessionId: e09b1b84-8f5f-4e3e-8d54-a3d4a992ff97
---
Always remove `.git/index.lock` without hesitation when it blocks git operations.

**Why:** Multi-agent QA+DEV loops frequently leave stale lock files when background agents crash or timeout mid-git-operation. The user runs parallel agents that all touch the same repo, so lock confli
